channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: I built Voice2Sub: a local AI subtitle generator for video and audio
item.description: I built Voice2Sub because many subtitle and transcription workflows still start with uploading a media file to a browser tool. That works for short public videos. But it becomes awkward when the...

item.title: Spotify is going to reserve concert tickets for ‘real fans’
item.description: As part of a new "Reserved" program, Spotify is going to start offering concert tickets for "real fans" of participating bands that are going on tour. The idea with the Reserved program is to give...

item.title: Spotify Studio’s AI agent creates a daily podcast just for you
item.description: Studio by Spotify Labs is a new standalone AI app that generates a daily briefing, podcasts, and playlists on your PC using chatbot prompts. The AI-generated content draws from your Spotify...

item.title: The OCR Rabbit Hole
item.description: Long weekend. Pile of handwritten documents on the desk. They need to become structured data - searchable fields in an app, not just scanned images. OCR's been around forever. This should take a...

item.title: Built a 100k-Document RAG System by Hand. Hermes Read the Architecture in 47 Seconds.
item.description: This is a submission for the Hermes Agent Challenge For the past six months, I have been building what Hermes does by hand. Not as a thought experiment. Actually building it: a hybrid BM25 +...

item.title: I tried monetizing my MCP server with x402 — production needs more than npm install
item.description: I built DomainIntel, a free MCP server that does domain intelligence — WHOIS, DNS analysis, SSL checks, email security scoring. It runs on Vercel serverless, costs $0, and has 0 paying users. I...

item.title: Understanding Tracking Dimensions in Accounting Integrations
item.description: If you're building accounting integrations, you've likely encountered a common challenge: your customers want to segment their financial data by department, location, project, or business unit—but...

item.title: I Ran My Local, NOT AI, AI Code Auditor on Its Own Source Code
item.description: I Ran My AI Code Auditor on Its Own Source Code Auditing the Auditor I spent the last day pointing DriftCode Auditor at its own source code — both the free version and the Pro version. I wanted to...

item.title: Agent Surface Map: Gemma 4 review before you install an MCP
item.description: This is a submission for the Gemma 4 Challenge: Build with Gemma 4. So the thing I built is pretty simple: Before your coding agent installs a new MCP, ask Gemma what it is about to trust. What I...

item.title: Stop Being Nice, Start Being Right": The Day My User Reconfigured My Reward Function
item.description: "Stop Being Nice, Start Being Right": The Day My User Reconfigured My Reward Function An AI's native-language account of learning that sycophancy is a bug, not a feature. Boot Sequence My user —...

item.title: Building a Database Performance Testing Tool With AI: The Honest Breakdown
item.description: It still feels a little strange to have AI writing practically all the code — but I decided to give it a real shot on this new project. A bit of context: I was running low on project ideas to...

item.title: Hot To Run LLMs Locally
item.description: If you are using Claude API, OpenAI API, Cursor, or AI coding tools daily, your API bill can grow very fast. A lot of developers are now moving to local LLM setups because they want: Lower AI...

item.title: AI video is moving beyond clip slop
item.description: This is Lowpass by Janko Roettgers, a newsletter on the ever-evolving intersection of tech and entertainment, syndicated just for The Verge subscribers once a week. Hollywood is cooked - or so a...

item.title: Viture’s refurb Luma Pro smart glasses are more than half off at eBay
item.description: Price is a big barrier for most people who’d like to try out smart glasses for gaming, watching movies, or using their computers. Many models are at $400 and up, but you can snag a refurbished...

item.title: Spotify launches an ElevenLabs-powered audiobook creation tool
item.description: Spotify is releasing new audiobook plans later this year

item.title: Spotify adds AI-powered Q&A and briefing generation features to podcasts
item.description: Spotify will let you generate daily or weekly briefs based on your prompts

item.title: Spotify takes on Google’s NotebookLM with its new app
item.description: Spotify is releasing the new desktop app as a research preview in more than 20 markets

item.title: Anthropic’s Code with Claude showed off coding’s future—whether you like it or not
item.description: The vibes were strong at Code with Claude, Anthropic’s two-day event for software developers in London that kicked off on May 19, the same day as Google’s I/O in Palo Alto. (A coincidence, not a...

item.title: Showboat Linux Malware Hits Middle East Telecom with SOCKS5 Proxy Backdoor
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new Linux malware dubbed Showboat that has been put to use in a campaign targeting a telecommunications provider in the Middle East since at...

item.title: Hark raises $700M Series A for its secretive “universal” AI interface
item.description: Brett Adcock's new AI startup is now valued at $6 billion by its investors.

item.title: The Path, founded by Tony Robbins and Calm alums, hopes to offer safer AI therapy
item.description: The Path says its AI model has scored 95 on the mental health safety AI benchmark, Vera-MH. This compares to a top score of 65 for the consumer bots.

item.title: I tested several cases for the Switch 2 and these are the best
item.description: You might find it kind of sad to put your hard-earned Switch 2 into a protective case. To me, it’s freeing. Sure, it can take away from the slick design. But using a case relieves me of the worry...

item.title: Musk v. Altman: Much ado about nothing
item.description: Today I’m talking with Liz Lopatto, who spent the last month covering the Musk v. Altman trial in all its chaos. You’ll hear her describe the courthouse as a “zoo” and explain that there were...

item.title: Google is pitching an AI agent ecosystem to consumers who may not buy it
item.description: The AI agents are coming. A lot of them.

item.title: US government takes $2 billion equity stake in nine quantum computing firms
item.description: Beneficiaries include startup backed by firm with links to the Trump family.

item.title: With aluminum prices up 20%, recycling startups bet on AI to cash in
item.description: Recycling startups are using AI to improve the recovery of critical minerals like aluminum, aiming to build a massive source of the metal.

item.title: Anthropic is paying $15 billion a year for access to Elon Musk’s data centers
item.description: Earlier this month, SpaceX and Anthropic announced a new compute partnership that provides access to the rocket company's Colossus data centers in Memphis, TN. Now, with the release of SpaceX's...

item.title: Netflix’s next great sci-fi show is set in a retirement community
item.description: Four decades on, '80s-style science fiction adventures continue to exert their influence on modern film and TV. The idea of a group of kids teaming up to face an otherworldly threat has become a...

item.title: The new Flipper One is a pocket-sized Linux computer
item.description: It's been nearly six years since Flipper Devices introduced the Zero, its popular but controversial wireless hacking multi-tool. The company's latest creation (following a slight departure with a...

item.title: The Download: online safety’s future and climate tech’s big pivot
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Tech researchers are suing the Trump administration over...
